name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-37
title: publish_settings + publish_hook ordering: stripping settings.json mid-install loses hooks
description: Wave 2 (rabbit-config audit) traced the false-alarm RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-100 (Plan D era) to an architectural quirk in contract.lib.publish: publish_settings does a destructive copy of source → .claude/settings.json, then publish_hook entries (declared later in rabbit-cage's MANIFEST) add hook registrations to that same file. If publish_settings runs WITHOUT the follow-on publish_hook calls — e.g., an operator invokes a partial verification flow or run_publish_loop is interrupted — .claude/settings.json is left in 'source-only' shape with no hooks registered. Subsequent Claude Code sessions then load a permissions-only settings.json and silently lose Stop/SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse dispatch. Repro: in a clean clone where .claude/settings.json HEAD has hooks, run only the publish_settings step of rabbit-cage's manifest (e.g., via direct publish.publish_settings invocation) and observe .claude/settings.json reverts to the rabbit-cage/settings.json source shape (no hooks). Wave 2's Inv 17 ensures rabbit-config's TESTS don't cause this (all use tempdirs), but the underlying publish-loop fragility remains. Fix candidates: (a) make publish_settings merge with the existing settings.json hooks section instead of overwriting; (b) document that publish_settings is non-atomic and MUST be followed by publish_hook within the same install loop; (c) restructure: publish_hook owns settings.json shape entirely and publish_settings becomes additive. Filed by Wave 2 backstop; defer to a focused contract.publish audit.

history.note: Backstop-fixed in commit 6c044afb: check_manifest_drift now walks all manifest entries, allowing publish_hook to run after publish_settings. Regression test wired.
